capabilities.title: Register a session
capabilities.how_we_test: We run the add command to register a new session, then read the saved registry back. We confirm the row exists with the title, tool, group, and folder we asked for.
capabilities.assertion: new registry row carries the given title, tool, group, and working directory

capabilities.title: Start a session
capabilities.how_we_test: We register a session and start it. We then ask the throwaway tmux server whether a live pane actually appeared, and confirm the registry flips the session to an active state.
capabilities.assertion: a real tmux pane appears on the isolated socket and status becomes active

capabilities.title: Stop a session
capabilities.how_we_test: We start a session, then stop it. We confirm the tmux pane is gone and the registry returns the session to the stopped state.
capabilities.assertion: tmux pane disappears and registry status returns to stopped

capabilities.title: Restart a session
capabilities.how_we_test: We start a session and restart it. We confirm exactly one pane exists afterward (no accidental duplicate) and the session is active again.
capabilities.assertion: exactly one pane remains after restart and status is active (guards the #30 double-spawn)

capabilities.title: Remove a session
capabilities.how_we_test: We confirm a stopped session can be removed and disappears from the registry, and that removing a still-running session is refused unless forced, so it is not destroyed by accident.
capabilities.assertion: stopped session leaves the registry; a running session is refused without force

capabilities.title: Launch in one step
capabilities.how_we_test: We use the single launch command, which creates, starts, and messages a session at once, pointed at the stand-in echo agent. We confirm the registry row exists and the echoed message shows up on screen.
capabilities.assertion: one launch command creates the row, starts the pane, and the echoed message appears

capabilities.title: Fork guard
capabilities.how_we_test: Forking is only valid for a Claude session with live context. We confirm forking a non-Claude session is cleanly refused and creates no orphan child row. The full context-inheriting fork is a documented nightly gap.
capabilities.assertion: forking a non-Claude session is refused and no child row is created

capabilities.title: Send a message to an agent and read its reply
capabilities.how_we_test: We launch a tiny stand-in agent that simply repeats whatever you say. We send it a unique message through the normal send command, then read the screen back. If the screen shows the echoed message we know it reached the agent and a reply came out the other side.
capabilities.assertion: the pane shows ECHO:<token> after a real send, proving readiness, send-keys, and capture read-back

capabilities.title: Real agent round trip (Claude)
capabilities.how_we_test: We launch a real Claude session, wait for its prompt, send a fixed instruction, and check the reply. This needs a real API key and network, so it runs nightly, not on every release.
capabilities.assertion: a real Claude reply contains the expected token

capabilities.title: Fork inherits Claude context
capabilities.how_we_test: We fork a live Claude session and confirm the child inherits the conversation with a distinct id and a parent link. This needs a real Claude session id from a live transcript, so it runs nightly.
capabilities.assertion: child session links the parent and inherits conversation context

capabilities.title: MCP actually loads in the agent
capabilities.how_we_test: We attach a tool server and ask a real agent to list its tools, confirming the agent honors the attachment. This needs a real agent to introspect, so it runs nightly.
capabilities.assertion: the agent lists the attached MCP server
